symbols: angel kneeling — reverence before the chosen vessel
Mary with book — reading Scripture at the moment of the announcement
checkered floor — domestic setting rendered with early perspective
description: A Trecento Sienese painting of the Annunciation set under Gothic arches with a rich gold background. The Angel Gabriel kneels at the left, wearing a pink-red robe with large wings, reaching toward or holding a scepter. The Virgin Mary sits at the right in a dark blue mantle, appearing to hold or read from a book. Both figures have golden halos. A small vase with a lily may be visible between them. The checkered floor shows an early attempt at spatial perspective. The style is characteristic of Ambrogio Lorenzetti's refined, elegant Sienese Gothic manner.
theological_description: This Annunciation follows the Sienese Gothic tradition of depicting the moment from Luke 1:26-38 when Gabriel announced to Mary that she would conceive the Son of God by the Holy Spirit. The gold background represents the irruption of the eternal into the temporal — heaven breaking into an ordinary home in Nazareth. Mary's book reflects the medieval tradition that she was reading Isaiah's prophecy of the virgin birth (Isaiah 7:14) at the very moment Gabriel arrived. The Gothic architectural setting frames the encounter as taking place in a sacred space, emphasizing that this domestic moment is the most important event in salvation history — the beginning of the Incarnation.

scripture_refs.reason: The Annunciation — Gabriel's announcement to Mary
The virgin shall conceive — the prophecy Mary was traditionally reading
The Word became flesh — the theological meaning of this moment
